pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 51, export_result.sh line 25, verifier.py line 59 all use identical string literal 'Marguerite'
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 22, task.json line 18, verifier.py line 47 all use identical string literal 'Pature des Saules'
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 37, task.json line 19 use identical string literal 'Etable Principale'

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data created during task setup
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh creates animal and zones using Rails create! method if they don't exist (lines 24-47, 54-66)

pi_items.verified_value: Real demo data from GAEC JOULIN (French farm) with 171 animals, 99 entities, etc.
pi_items.source: Environment evidence documentation (evidence_docs/README.md lines 7, 47-58) shows the base environment loads real demo data, but this task creates synthetic test data on top

privileged_info_summary: The task 'record_animal_movement@1' uses synthetic test data: an animal named Marguerite and two zones named 'Pature des Saules' (target) and 'Etable Principale' (start) are created during task setup via Rails if they do not already exist. These string identifiers are consistently used across all task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py). The underlying Ekylibre environment (version 4.24.0) uses real demo data from GAEC JOULIN (a real French farm), but this specific task layer introduces synthetic data for testing purposes.
